title: POST /api/auth/login — authenticate user and return JWT
description: Implement the login endpoint that validates email/password credentials and returns a JWT token for authenticated sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /api/auth/login with valid credentials returns 200 with access_token and user object
Wrong password returns 401 with message 'Invalid email or password'
Unknown email returns 401 with message 'Invalid email or password'
Returned JWT can be used to access protected endpoints

steps.details: In src/api/schemas/auth.py, add LoginRequest(BaseModel) with fields: email (EmailStr), password (str). Reuse the existing AuthResponse schema for the response.

In src/api/routers/auth.py, add POST /login route. Steps: query db for User where User.email == request.email, if not found raise HTTPException(401, 'Invalid email or password'), call verify_password(request.password, user.hashed_password), if False raise HTTPException(401, 'Invalid email or password'), create JWT with create_access_token({'sub': user.id}), return AuthResponse.

POST to http://localhost:8000/api/auth/login with {"email": "test@example.com", "password": "password123"}. Verify: status 200, access_token present. Then test with wrong password — verify 401. Then test with unknown email — verify 401. Both error cases should return the same generic message to prevent user enumeration.

technicalNotes: Always return the same error message for wrong email and wrong password to prevent user enumeration attacks
Do not add a sleep/delay to prevent timing attacks — bcrypt comparison is already slow enough
